name: apify-ai-sandbox
title: Apify AI Sandbox
description: Apify AI Sandbox Actor that allows execution of code and operations in a containerized environment for AI coding agents

input.title: Actor input schema
input.description:
AI Sandbox for running AI coding operations in a containerized environment. 🚀

## How to use
* **Connect with MCP client:** Use a Model Context Protocol (MCP) client to interact with this sandbox. See https://modelcontextprotocol.io/clients
* **Use Apify SDK:** Connect programmatically using the Apify SDK for direct integration
* **REST API:** Access the sandbox directly via REST API endpoints

## Configuration
* **Memory & timeout:** Configure run options below to set memory allocation and execution timeout
* **Check logs:** Open the Actor run log console to view connection details and operation output

skills.title: Skills
skills.description: Skill packages to install for the AI coding agent — SKILLS.md files with specialized instructions (see https://skills.sh/). One skill per line, e.g. `anthropics/skills` (blank lines and `#` comments are ignored), or a JSON array like `["apify/agent-skills", "anthropics/skills"]`.
skills.prefill:
apify/agent-skills
# One skill per line, e.g. anthropics/skills

nodeDependencies.title: Node.js dependencies
nodeDependencies.description: npm packages for JavaScript/TypeScript code execution (/sandbox/js-ts). One `package@version` per line (omit `@version` for `latest`; blank lines and `#` comments are ignored), or a JSON object like `{"zod": "^3.0", "axios": "latest"}`.
nodeDependencies.prefill:
# One package@version per line (omit @version for latest):
# zod@^3.0
# axios

pythonRequirementsTxt.title: Python requirements
pythonRequirementsTxt.description: Python packages for Python code execution (/sandbox/py), in requirements.txt format — one package per line.
pythonRequirementsTxt.prefill:
# One package per line (requirements.txt format):
# requests==2.31.0
# pandas

initShellScript.title: Initialization script
initShellScript.description: Optional bash script run on startup, after dependencies are installed. Use it to install system packages, create directories, or set up the environment.
initShellScript.prefill:
#!/bin/bash
# Runs on startup. Uncomment or add your own:
# apt-get update && apt-get install -y jq
# mkdir -p /sandbox/data

envVars.title: Environment variables
envVars.description:
Secret env vars exposed **only to the init bash script**, then deleted before the shell, MCP server, and code execution start. Encrypted at rest by Apify.

Accepts `KEY=VALUE` per line or a JSON object, e.g. `{"GITHUB_TOKEN": "ghp_..."}`.

idleTimeoutSeconds.title: Idle timeout seconds
idleTimeoutSeconds.description: The container shuts down automatically after this many seconds of inactivity (no requests or shell interaction). Set to 0 to disable. Recommended: set the Actor timeout to 0 (infinite) when using this.

proxyMappings.title: Proxy mappings
proxyMappings.description: Map external paths to local web servers. Example: [{"path": "/myapp", "target": "http://127.0.0.1:3000/myapp"}]

target.title: Local service URL
target.description: Full URL of local service (e.g., http://127.0.0.1:18789/openclaw)

mcpConnections.title: MCP Connections
mcpConnections.description: MCP Connectors the sandbox can call on your behalf (Slack, Notion, GitHub, or any MCP server authorized in Settings > API & Integrations > MCP Connectors). At runtime the platform injects your credentials, exposes each as a proxy at `${APIFY_MCP_PROXY_URL}/<connectorId>`, and writes the list to `/sandbox/mcp.json` so tools like `mcpc connect` pick them up immediately.
